reasoning:
The stock price drift over small horizons is essentially a random walk with high variance. However, VICI pays a relatively large dividend (~$0.4325 per quarter on a ~$30 stock, roughly 1.4%). Due to the way unadjusted stock prices work, the price will mechanically drop by this amount on the ex-dividend date. VICI announced a June 18, 2025 record/ex-dividend date. 

- For D1 (2025-06-15), the market closes on June 13, before the ex-dividend drop. Short-term trend from the tool was slightly bearish, so probability is a near toss-up at 0.48.
- For D2 (2025-07-08), the stock will have suffered the ~1.4% dividend drop without enough time to recover via expected market drift. This significantly disadvantages the 'higher' outcome, reducing the probability to 0.45.
- For D3 (2025-09-06), the Q3 dividend historically goes ex-dividend around Sept 18, meaning only ONE dividend drop has occurred by this date. ~3 months of expected positive market drift should roughly offset the single drop, raising the estimate to 0.48.
- For D4 (2025-12-05), two dividend drops will have happened, but with ~6 months of upward drift, expected excess return is slightly positive. Crucially, the variance over a 6-month period is so high that structural factors are swamped by noise, meaning the probability must converge strongly toward the uncertainty maximum of 0.50.
